assertiveness_why: Harry surfaces a listener-submitted question and directly asks if Founder Collective's small checks and no-follow-on strategy leaves money on the table, pressing Eric to defend the model against the obvious counterargument.
question_style: Largely open narrative questions but with a few pointed challenges relayed from other VCs (David Hornick, implicitly others) asking Eric to justify small check sizes and the no-pro-rata strategy; Harry occasionally goes off-script ('this isn't on the schedule at all') to follow a thread deeper.
tone: Warm, admiring, conversational; Harry expresses genuine enthusiasm and breaks format spontaneously, showing curiosity rather than pure fandom.
depth: Goes past surface into fund strategy mechanics -- pro-rata economics, cost-basis dollar-averaging, ownership vs. capital efficiency -- and probes the human/emotional side of being a seed investor.
harry_quotes: Is that the general ticket size that you traditionally put in?
Do you feel that you miss out then financially by not doing follow-on rounds?
Does it slightly irritate you to see so many VCs call themselves founder friendly?
learnings: Founder Collective avoids pro-rata rights because they see it as structurally misaligned: the fund only wants to exercise the option when the company is struggling, which works against the founder.
Concentrating checks at the seed stage rather than following on preserves a lower average cost basis across the portfolio instead of creeping toward Series B pricing.
Even without full pro-rata participation, a small early check in a massive outlier like Uber can still move the fund's entire return profile.
Paley views venture returns as fundamentally an outlier-driven business, so judging a strategy by removing its outliers misrepresents how the model actually works.
repeat_guest_callback: We just interviewed. / who we just interviewed (referencing David Frankel's episode earlier that week)
notable: Second episode of a linked two-part Founder Collective feature, directly cross-referencing the David Frankel episode from earlier the same week.
